A firm must notify the PRA immediately in accordance with Notifications 7 where the nature or quality of reinsurance relied on to reduce the required margin of solvency materially changes duri
This Chapter only applies to an incorporated friendly society.
A firm must ensure that its margin of solvency does not fall below the guarantee fund.
The guarantee fund is an amount equal to the greater of:
In the case of long-term insurance business , a firm must maintain a margin of solvency (excluding implicit items), that are sufficient to cover the greater of:
In the case of general insurance business , the unpaid initial fund of a firm and, in the case of a firm with variable contributions, any claim which the firm has against its members by way
In the case of long-term insurance business , the unpaid initial fund of a firm and implicit items which relate to future profits and zillmerising may not be taken into account in complying

sections.chapter_title: Minimum Guarantee Fund: Long-Term Insurance Business

rules.summary: Subject to 6.2, the minimum guarantee fund for long-term insurance business is the amou
If a firm has not been in existence long enough to have a preceding financial year , the minimum guarantee fund for long-term insurance business is £90,000.

sections.chapter_title: Minimum Guarantee Fund: General Insurance Business

rules.summary: The minimum guarantee fund for general insurance business is £210,000.
A firm must take account of 8.2 to [8.6](/pra-rules/friendly-society---overall-resources-
A firm with variable contributions, carrying on general insurance business , must treat any claim which the firm has against its members by way of a call for supplementary contributions for a _f
A firm must treat implicit items as having no value.
A firm must treat an unpaid initial fund as having no value.
